---
title: "Data collection"
output: pdf_document
---


Victim representativeness
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)

df <- read_csv("Fixed_Date_version.csv")

# Variable types - Look for incorrect types
sapply(df, class)

# Victim age distribution

subset_df <- df[df$`Vict Age` > 0 & df$`Vict Age` <= 100,] # Too large of outliers were included

summary(subset_df$`Vict Age`)

ggplot(subset_df, aes(x = `Vict Age`)) +
  geom_bar() + scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(subset_df$`Vict Age`), max(subset_df$`Vict Age`), by = 3))

# Victim sex distribution

table(subset_df$`Vict Sex`)

ggplot(subset_df, aes(x = `Vict Sex`)) +
  geom_bar()

# Victim race/ethnicity distribution
table(df$`Vict Descent`)
ggplot(subset_df, aes(x = `Vict Descent`)) +
  geom_bar()

```



split data by year
```{r}
df_2020 <- df[df$`DATE OCC` < as.POSIXct("2021-01-01"),]

df_2021 <- df[df$`DATE OCC` < as.POSIXct("2022-01-01") & df$`DATE OCC` >= as.POSIXct("2021-01-01"),]

df_2022 <- df[df$`DATE OCC` < as.POSIXct("2023-01-01") & df$`DATE OCC` >= as.POSIXct("2022-01-01"),]

df_2023 <- df[df$`DATE OCC` < as.POSIXct("2024-01-01") & df$`DATE OCC` >= as.POSIXct("2023-01-01"),]

df_2024 <- df[df$`DATE OCC` < as.POSIXct("2025-01-01") & df$`DATE OCC` >= as.POSIXct("2024-01-01"),]
```

Non response rates per year for age, sex and ethnicity
```{r}
# Age non response rates
age_rate_2020 <- sum(is.na(df_2020$`Vict Age`)) / nrow(df_2020)
age_rate_2021 <- sum(is.na(df_2021$`Vict Age`)) / nrow(df_2021)
age_rate_2022 <- sum(is.na(df_2022$`Vict Age`)) / nrow(df_2022)
age_rate_2023 <- sum(is.na(df_2023$`Vict Age`)) / nrow(df_2023)
age_rate_2024 <- sum(is.na(df_2024$`Vict Age`)) / nrow(df_2024)
avg_age_rate <- (age_rate_2020 + age_rate_2021 + age_rate_2022 + age_rate_2023 + age_rate_2024) / 5

cat("Non-response rate for Age from 2020: ", age_rate_2020, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for Age from 2021: ", age_rate_2021, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for Age from 2022: ", age_rate_2022, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for Age from 2023: ", age_rate_2023, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for Age from 2024: ", age_rate_2024, "\n" )
cat("Average Non-response rate per year for age", avg_age_rate, "\n")
cat("\n")

# Sex non response rates
sex_rate_2020 <- sum(is.na(df_2020$`Vict Sex`)) / nrow(df_2020)
sex_rate_2021 <- sum(is.na(df_2021$`Vict Sex`)) / nrow(df_2021)
sex_rate_2022 <- sum(is.na(df_2022$`Vict Sex`)) / nrow(df_2022)
sex_rate_2023 <- sum(is.na(df_2023$`Vict Sex`)) / nrow(df_2023)
sex_rate_2024 <- sum(is.na(df_2024$`Vict Sex`)) / nrow(df_2024)
avg_sex_rate <- (sex_rate_2020 + sex_rate_2021 + sex_rate_2022 + sex_rate_2023 + sex_rate_2024) / 5

cat("Non-response rate for sex from 2020: ", sex_rate_2020, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for sex from 2021: ", sex_rate_2021, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for sex from 2022: ", sex_rate_2022, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for sex from 2023: ", sex_rate_2023, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for sex from 2024: ", sex_rate_2024, "\n" )
cat("Average Non-response rate per year for sex", avg_sex_rate, "\n")
cat("\n")

# Ethinicity response rates
race_rate_2020 <- sum(is.na(df_2020$`Vict Descent`)) / nrow(df_2020)
race_rate_2021 <- sum(is.na(df_2021$`Vict Descent`)) / nrow(df_2021)
race_rate_2022 <- sum(is.na(df_2022$`Vict Descent`)) / nrow(df_2022)
race_rate_2023 <- sum(is.na(df_2023$`Vict Descent`)) / nrow(df_2023)
race_rate_2024 <- sum(is.na(df_2024$`Vict Descent`)) / nrow(df_2024)
avg_race_rate <- (race_rate_2020 + race_rate_2021 + race_rate_2022 + race_rate_2023 + race_rate_2024) / 5

cat("Non-response rate for race from 2020: ", race_rate_2020, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for race from 2021: ", race_rate_2021, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for race from 2022: ", race_rate_2022, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for race from 2023: ", race_rate_2023, "\n" )
cat("Non-response rate for race from 2024: ", race_rate_2024, "\n" )
cat("Average Non-response rate per year for race", avg_race_rate, "\n")

```

Results - Information relevant in helping to answer research question one.

```{r}
# Understanding crime codes to identify violent crimes
# Recall that crime codes 1 are primary, second is an additional secondary crime, third is an additional third crime, and fourth it fourth where every 1 is most violent and 4th is least
number_of_crime_codes_1 <- length(unique(df$`Crm Cd`))
number_of_crime_codes_2 <- length(unique(df$`Crm Cd 2`))
number_of_crime_codes_3 <- length(unique(df$`Crm Cd 3`))
number_of_crime_codes_4 <- length(unique(df$`Crm Cd 4`))

cat("Unique crime codes in:", number_of_crime_codes_1, "\n")
cat("Unique crime codes in:", number_of_crime_codes_2,"\n")
cat("Unique crime codes in:", number_of_crime_codes_3, "\n")
cat("Unique crime codes in:", number_of_crime_codes_4,"\n")
cat("\n")
violent_codes <- c(110, 113, 121, 122, 210, 220, 230, 231, 235, 236, 250, 251, 434, 435, 436, 437, 622, 623, 624, 625, 626, 627, 647, 753, 755, 756, 760, 761, 763, 810, 812, 815, 820, 821, 822, 860, 910, 920, 921, 922, 926, 928, 930, 940, 943)

violent_df <- df[df$`Crm Cd` %in% violent_codes | df$`Crm Cd 2` %in% violent_codes, ]

df_2020 <- violent_df[violent_df$`DATE OCC` < as.POSIXct("2021-01-01"),]

df_2021 <- violent_df[violent_df$`DATE OCC` < as.POSIXct("2022-01-01") & violent_df$`DATE OCC` >= as.POSIXct("2021-01-01"),]

df_2022 <- violent_df[violent_df$`DATE OCC` < as.POSIXct("2023-01-01") & violent_df$`DATE OCC` >= as.POSIXct("2022-01-01"),]

df_2023 <- violent_df[violent_df$`DATE OCC` < as.POSIXct("2024-01-01") & violent_df$`DATE OCC` >= as.POSIXct("2023-01-01"),]

df_2024 <- violent_df[violent_df$`DATE OCC` < as.POSIXct("2025-01-01") & violent_df$`DATE OCC` >= as.POSIXct("2024-01-01"),]

sum_2020 <- nrow(df_2020)
sum_2021 <- nrow(df_2021)
sum_2022 <- nrow(df_2022)
sum_2023 <- nrow(df_2023)
sum_2024 <- nrow(df_2024)

cat("Number of violent crimes for 2020:", sum_2020, "\n")
cat("Number of violent crimes for 2021:", sum_2021, "\n")
cat("Number of violent crimes for 2022:", sum_2022, "\n")
cat("Number of violent crimes for 2023:", sum_2023, "\n")
cat("Number of violent crimes for 2024:", sum_2024, "\n")
cat("\n")

# Area distribution

ggplot(subset_df, aes(x = AREA)) +
  geom_bar() + scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(subset_df$`Vict Age`), max(subset_df$`Vict Age`), by = 3)) + labs(title = "Crime Counts per Area (2020â€“2024)")

print("Table of crimes per area from 2020-2024")
table(df$AREA)


```
